channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Dining and Flex Dollars Reminder
item.description: Friendly reminder to all students with active residential meal plans that the last day to purchase additional flex dollars for the Spring 2026 semester will be April 27th. The last day to utilize Flex Dollars for Spring 2026 meal plans is May 16th — please keep in mind that flex dollar balances do not carry over at the end of the academic year. Dining Halls close for the Spring 2026 semester on May 8th after dinner. For more information click the link above.
item.author: Students

item.title: Data Centers: The Next Industrial Revolution, Current & Future, Challenges & Opps
item.description: Dr. Peter De Bock is currently Vice President, Data Center Energy and Liquid Cooling Technology at Eaton, where he is responsible for shaping strategy, innovation, and customer engagement across one of the fastest-growing and most critical segments of the global digital infrastructure market.
item.author: Students

item.title: Returning Housing Application Preferences Closing!
item.description: The ability for returning students to list their roommate and housing preferences will close at 5:00pm on April 24th. Please make sure your roommate groups are verified in the housing portal or they will not be considered.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Review Sessions Week of 4/26-5/3
item.description: -Diff Eq: Sunday, 4/26 (3-5pm) at the Success Center -Math Methods: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) at ECAV: Harkness Room (Room 3211) -Data Management: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) at ECAV: Championship Room (Room 3130) -Data Structures: Monday, 4/27 (8-10pm) at the Success Center -CAL II: Monday, 4/27 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom
item.author: Students

item.title: Corpus Festival | April 23-25, 2026
item.description: A weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. How do we take part in society by physically showing up? We all play our part in shaping power, control, freedom, and mutual responsibility.
item.author: Community

item.title: Starman: Screening & Filmmaker Q&A
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 4:30 PM - 7:00 PM - Join us for a screening of Starman, a New York Times Critics Pick. In this meditative, intergalactic biopic from Academy Award®-nominated director Robert Stone, we follow Gentry Lee, Chief Engineer for Planetary Exploration at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, on his remarkable journey through space and on Earth. Followed by a live Q&A with Robert Stone, moderated by Dr. William Gibbons, Dean, School of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences…
item.author: Community

item.title: Connected Dreams: Advanced Digital Imaging Exhibition
item.description: You are cordially invited to Connected Dreams, an exhibition of our Advanced Digital Imaging class short study works in progress. The exhibition is in the Sage Labs Dean’s Gallery and extends to the Vertical Galleries (near the elevators on each floor) and sections of the Corridors of Creativity near Sage 2411. Please bring your mobile devices to experience the AR.
item.author: Community

item.title: Mozart Violin Sonatas Concert
item.description: Join us this Sunday April 26, 2026, for a concert with Mozart violin sonatas, at 6:30 pm at the HASS Media Center (DCC 174). The concert is a collaboration between two instrumental studios at RPI, Jamecyn Morey’s violin and Chrysi Nanou’s piano studios and the Chamber Music Ensembles class.
item.author: Community

item.title: Additional Commencement Ticket Information
item.description: On Friday, April 24th, starting at 9:00 AM EDT, students who registered for Commencement can claim one additional ticket. Please note that there are only 500 tickets available due to the number of graduates and the venue’s capacity. To claim your additional ticket, log in to your Tassel account and return to the registration form to the “your tickets” page. If you have any questions, please reach out to commencement@rpi.edu.
item.author: Community

item.title: Queering RPI | Collecting Positive Queer Experiences on Campus
item.description: Queering RPI is a storytelling and community building project designed to highlight the experiences, memories, and perspectives of queer students, faculty, and staff at RPI. The goal is to show students that they are not alone, that their identities matter, and that there is space for them on campus. This project collects and shares anonymous anecdotes, reflections, and moments from queer members of the RPI community.
item.author: Community

item.title: North American Conference on Video Game Music
item.description: The North American Conference on Video Game Music (NACVGM) returns May 1–2, 2026 as a hybrid event hosted at RPI at EMPAC. The conference features a dynamic program of scholarly papers, panels, and creative work, alongside performances and opportunities for community engagement. Participants present 20- and 10-minute talks, poster sessions, and demonstrations of game and sound art, reflecting the breadth of contemporary approaches to game audio research and practice.
item.author: Community

item.title: TVCOG RPI Night | April 28
item.description: Join the RPI community for an evening at the Tech Valley Center of Gravity, sponsored by the RPI Lally School of Management, from 6–8 PM. Explore a 14,000 sq. ft. makerspace, connect with local alumni, and enjoy free pizza. Open to all students and located near the Blitman shuttle stop. RSVP via the link above.
item.author: Community

item.title: Join the MANE Student Advisor Council for Cookies, Coffee, and Questions
item.description: Date: Friday, April 24, 2026 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM - Join the MANE Student Advisory Council (SAC) for Cookies, Coffee, and Questins! Provide the MANE Department feedback on the Spring 2026 semester to get free cookies and coffee! Table will be located on the second floor of the JEC.
item.author: Community

item.title: Volunteers Needed: Late Night Breakfast
item.description:
We’re looking for volunteers on April 30th from 8:30 pm - 11 pm to help make Late Night Breakfast a success! Volunteers will assist with greeting students, serving food and helping to keep the good vibes going! If you’re interested in helping out or would like more information please email auxiliaryservices@rpi.edu. We’d love to have you on the team!

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
